title: AFI Credential Reference v1 (FACTORY-CONTRACT — opaque, non-secret BYOK pointer)
description: A non-secret, opaque, scoped POINTER to credentials (afi.credential-ref.v1). A CredentialRef carries only what is needed to resolve a credential safely at runtime: an OPAQUE deployment-local reference id (never a value, never a backend path), the tenant/operator scope that owns it, the provider it is compatible with, the credential KIND, a record version, and an active/disabled status. It NEVER contains an API key, bearer token, password, private key, session cookie, OAuth refresh token, complete authorization header, or any secret payload. The actual secret lives in a deployment secret backend keyed by (tenant, credentialRef); rotation and revocation happen BEHIND this reference and are invisible to any artifact. This object is a fixture/reference shape only — deployment-specific credential-reference records are operator configuration, not a public registry (PBF-GOV D-PBF-7).
$comment: BOUNDARY (FACTORY-CONTRACT): schema/contract only — NO secret storage, NO secret manager, NO backend topology. additionalProperties:false plus explicit allowed fields is the primary defense (an inline apiKey/token/secret/password/authorization field has nowhere to live); the BYOK denylist validator additionally rejects secret-named fields as defense in depth. The reference id is opaque and MUST NOT encode a cloud project, Secret Manager path, filesystem location, URL, or any backend location.
x-afiPartOf: AFI Provider Adapter & BYOK Foundations v0.1 (PBF-GOV)
x-afiDoctrineRefs: afi-governance/decisions/provider-byok-foundations-v0.1 (PBF-GOV) D-PBF-4/D-PBF-7 — CredentialRef is opaque, scoped, and contains no secret value; runtime resolution is least-privilege and scoped to the active provider instance
afi-governance/decisions/factory-configurable-pipelines-v1 (FCP-GOV) D-FCP-5 — conforming credential references are administrative deployment updates

x-afiConstraints.noSecretMaterial: PRIMARY: additionalProperties:false plus the explicit allowed-field set means an inline apiKey/api_key/apikey/token/accessToken/secret/password/authorization/privateKey/refreshToken/cookie field is rejected as an unknown property — a CredentialRef has no field for a secret value. SECONDARY: the BYOK denylist validator walks all keys against the secret-name denylist and rejects any match. The reference is opaque: the actual secret is resolved out-of-band by a least-privilege SecretResolver keyed by (tenant, credentialRef).
x-afiConstraints.opaqueReference: credentialRef is a deployment-local opaque id (lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens). It MUST NOT be a URL, filesystem path, Secret Manager path, cloud project name, or any backend location — the backend mapping is deployment-local and never exposed here.
x-afiConstraints.providerCompatibility: CROSS-FIELD (registry-tooling): providerId MUST name an existing afi.provider.v1 that requiresCredential, and credentialKind MUST equal that provider's credentialKind. Enforced by the provider-BYOK cross-reference tests.
x-afiConstraints.rotationBehindReference: Rotation is a secret-backend operation invisible to this record; status 'disabled' revokes without deletion.

schema.description: Schema-id version of the credential reference.

credentialRef.description: OPAQUE deployment-local reference id — NOT a secret value, NOT a backend path (x-afiConstraints.opaqueReference). The actual secret lives in a deployment secret backend keyed by (tenant, credentialRef).

recordVersion.description: Semantic version of THIS reference record (no v prefix). The secret it points to rotates behind the reference without changing this version.

tenant.description: Tenant/operator scope that owns this credential reference. A resolver resolves a reference only for its owning tenant (least-privilege, D-PBF-7).

providerId.description: The provider (afi.provider.v1 providerId) this credential is compatible with. MUST name a credentialed provider (x-afiConstraints.providerCompatibility).

credentialKind.description: Kind of credential this reference resolves to; MUST equal the provider's credentialKind. Names the kind only — never a value.

status.description: 'active' resolves; 'disabled' revokes without deletion. Rotation/revocation are secret-backend operations behind the reference.
